Toggle navigation Zymoseptoria tritici (SEPTTR) Menu Overview → Photos Documents Overview Code created in: 2002-03-14 Basic information EPPO Code: SEPTTR Preferred name: Zymoseptoria tritici Authority: (Desmazières) Quaedvlieg & Crous Notes One of the top ten fungi in molecular plant pathology. Other scientific names Name Authority Mycosphaerella graminicola (Fuckel) Schröter Septoria graminum Desmazières Septoria tritici Berkeley & Curtis Common names Name Language leaf spot of wheat English septoria leaf blotch English septoria leaf spot English speckled leaf blotch of wheat English Blattdürre: Weizen German Blattfleckenkrankheit: Weizen German Schwarzfleckigkeit: Weizen German Septoria-Blattdürre German septoriose du blé French tache septorienne du blé French seca del trigo Spanish septoriosis del trigo Spanish svartpricksjuka Swedish септориоз пшеницы Russian hvedegråplet Danish septorioza paskowana lisci Polish lapų septoriozė Lithuanian more photos... Taxonomy Kingdom Fungi      (
      1FUNGK      ) Phylum Ascomycota      (
      1ASCOP      ) Subphylum Pezizomycotina      (
      1PEZIQ      ) Class Dothideomycetes      (
      1DOTHC      ) Subclass Dothideomycetidae      (
      1DTHDL      ) Order Mycosphaerellales      (
      1MYCPO      ) Family Mycosphaerellaceae      (
      1MYCOF      ) Genus Zymoseptoria      (
      1ZYMSG      ) Species Zymoseptoria tritici      (
      SEPTTR      )

seed.symptoms: Small circular spots with dark brown margins, gray-white centers, and tiny black pycnidia on lower leaves first; rapid upward spread causing defoliation.
seed.causes: Fungus Septoria lycopersici; overwinters on infected debris and solanaceous weeds; spreads via rain splash during warm wet weather.
seed.treatment: Fungicides (chlorothalonil, mancozeb, copper) applied preventively every 7-10 days; remove infected lower leaves.
seed.prevention: Crop rotation; mulching to reduce splash; stake plants for airflow; destroy crop debris after harvest.
